definition: The mind as an experiential and affective center that can be trained, collected, liberated, and observed.
notes: The project prefers mind because it is the cleanest default in contemporary English. Heart-mind remains acceptable in explanatory prose when the affective coloration matters, but consciousness should be avoided because that territory is already occupied by viññāṇa in this dataset. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji often reads citta as mood or affective process in meditation instruction, especially in MN 118-style material; the repository records those as source-backed pedagogical alternates while retaining mind as the house default. The OSF glossary describes citta as mind and heart-mind, the center of consciousness; the repository records center of consciousness as a source-backed explanatory alternate while keeping mind as the default.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and practical contexts
In explanatory prose where emotional tone and cognition are both clearly in play
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only when the fuller nuance helps.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style meditation instruction emphasizing shifting affective tone
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

context_rules.context: In OSF-glossary style explanatory prose about the affective center of experience
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

example_phrases.translation: one remains observing the mind in relation to the mind
